value.title: Treat BrandBuild and AI Video as the same product family
value.decision: Allow BrandBuild and AI Video Studio naming to coexist as one product family rather than treating them as separate repos or products.
value.reason: User explicitly said AI Video and BrandBuild are the same.
alternatives_considered: Force a separate-product interpretation
Split responsibilities across different repos
value.impact: Repo cleanup and roadmap decisions focus on removing unrelated product work instead of renaming everything immediately.

value.title: Remove unrelated legacy runtime source from BrandBuild
value.decision: Keep the canonical BrandBuild repo focused on BrandBuild surfaces and block reintroduction of unrelated legacy runtime features.
value.reason: The repo had already experienced cross-product contamination and needed stronger boundaries.
alternatives_considered: Allow mixed product runtime features to coexist in the repo
Delay cleanup until after future feature work
value.impact: BrandBuild stays cleaner, and future work can build on a more trustworthy surface.

value.title: Use .project-memory as the initial durable memory location
value.decision: Store the first repo-backed project memory set inside .project-memory at repo root.
value.reason: It was the fastest way to initialize durable memory inside the repo during cleanup work.
alternatives_considered: docs/project-memory
Only human-facing markdown files at project-memory
value.impact: Project memory existed immediately, but duplicate memory locations later needed consolidation.

value.title: Send BrandBuild cleanup upstream on main
value.decision: Push the BrandBuild cleanup and memory initialization upstream on main rather than leaving it local-only.
value.reason: User explicitly asked to send all changed repos.
alternatives_considered: Leave the cleanup local-only
Delay the push until all naming drift was resolved
value.impact: The canonical repo became safer to build on without waiting for every later cleanup.

value.title: Expose system readiness inside the product
value.decision: Add an in-product settings and control-center surface that shows integration readiness, architecture gaps, and next actions.
value.reason: The product needed a visible operator-facing truth layer instead of leaving audit state buried in code and chat.
alternatives_considered: Keep readiness knowledge only in docs
Wait until all integrations were finished before exposing status
value.impact: BrandBuild now has a clearer internal operating surface at /dashboard/settings.

value.title: Canonicalize structured memory in docs/project-memory
value.decision: Use docs/project-memory as the canonical structured memory layer, mirror it into .project-memory to avoid stale loads, and keep project-memory as the human-facing operating summary requested by the user.
value.reason: The repo had diverged across three memory locations and needed a single structured source of truth without destroying history.
alternatives_considered: Keep .project-memory canonical
Delete the duplicate folders outright
value.impact: Future sessions can reconstruct state reliably while still preserving the earlier memory location.

value.title: Use shot-grouped compare outputs as the reviews surface
value.decision: Keep /dashboard/reviews as the compare route and rebuild it around shot-grouped candidates with media previews, provider context, and focused review details rather than adding a separate compare route.
value.reason: The product needed a first-class comparison workflow quickly, and the existing reviews surface already owned selection logic and review context.
alternatives_considered: Keep the existing flat review list and defer compare UX
Create a brand new /dashboard/compare route before improving the reviews workspace
value.impact: BrandBuild now expresses its multi-model value proposition much more clearly without changing the route structure or schema.

value.title: Use /dashboard/assets as the versions and handoff workspace
value.decision: Keep /dashboard/assets as the unified route for asset browsing, version groups, and handoff preparation instead of introducing a separate versions or deliverables route at this stage.
value.reason: The assets surface already owned the mental model for outputs and files, so extending it into versions and handoff kept navigation stable while making the workflow materially more useful.
alternatives_considered: Create a separate /dashboard/versions route before the model was mature
Wait to build handoff until a full timeline editor existed
value.impact: BrandBuild now has a functional pre-edit delivery layer without route churn or schema changes, and the next missing step is sequencing rather than structure.

value.title: Send all public access CTAs directly to login
value.decision: Route the public-site header and footer access CTAs straight to /login and make decorative marketing layers non-interactive so access buttons stay reliably clickable across desktop and mobile.
value.reason: The public site should never depend on a protected /dashboard redirect for the basic sign-in path, and decorative overlays should not be allowed to interfere with navigation hit-testing.
alternatives_considered: Keep the header primary CTA pointed at /dashboard and rely on middleware redirects
Only change the button label without changing click behavior
Leave decorative overlays interactive and assume browser hit-testing would remain reliable
value.impact: Public access is clearer on small screens, login intent is explicit, and the marketing shell is less likely to swallow taps or clicks that should navigate into authentication.

value.title: Use an API route for campaign creation instead of the brittle server-action boundary
value.decision: Move campaign creation onto POST /api/campaigns and let the create-campaign modal handle success and error states client-side instead of relying on a server-action path that could throw users into the global error screen.
value.reason: Campaign creation was still producing a full-page failure state for the user, and the server-action boundary made the failure mode harder to contain and explain inline.
alternatives_considered: Keep the server-action flow and keep patching thrown errors around it
Disable campaign creation until the dashboard auth and persistence stack were revisited more broadly
value.impact: Campaign creation now fails inline through JSON responses, redirects directly to the new campaign on success, and is easier to debug and validate separately from the page shell.

value.title: Expose storage readiness truth and validate providers with a dedicated smoke harness
value.decision: Keep the private-upload requirement strict, but surface the blocked state clearly in the shot uploader and add a reusable provider smoke-test script so real provider readiness can be validated without pretending uploads are available when storage is not configured.
value.reason: The live deployment is missing SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, and users need a truthful product state plus a repeatable way to validate Sora, Kling, and Higgsfield from the repo.
alternatives_considered: Leave the uploader looking available and let the route fail with a generic message
Hide all upload surfaces entirely whenever storage credentials are missing
Rely on ad hoc shell commands instead of a committed smoke-test tool
value.impact: Operators now get explicit upload-readiness messaging, the settings audit reflects the real blocker, and the repo has a reusable control script for provider integration checks.

value.title: Proxy completed Sora media through BrandBuild
value.decision: Completed Sora outputs are exposed to operators through /api/generate/[generationId]/content instead of relying on a missing url field from the OpenAI video status object.
value.reason: OpenAI video status responses can be completed without containing a direct media URL. The actual media lives behind an authenticated /content endpoint, so BrandBuild needs its own server-side proxy to make completed outputs usable in the UI.
alternatives_considered: Keep treating the status object url as authoritative even when it is missing.
Expose the raw OpenAI /content endpoint directly to the client.
Delay all Sora result viewing until storage mirroring is configured.
value.impact: Generation history and future asset views can link to completed Sora media reliably, and the provider abstraction now matches the real OpenAI video retrieval model.

value.title: Move shot uploads to authenticated storage policies
value.decision: BrandBuild shot uploads and signed asset URLs should use the authenticated Supabase client plus storage policies, while SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY remains optional for backend-only provider-output mirroring.
value.reason: Requiring the service-role key for basic operator uploads made the product unnecessarily brittle and blocked the workflow on otherwise healthy deployments.
alternatives_considered: Keep all storage operations behind the admin client.
Make uploads public instead of private.
Disable uploads until service-role credentials are always present.
value.impact: Private uploads can work with normal authenticated operator sessions once the assets bucket and storage policies are applied, and provider-output mirroring remains an additive backend capability.
